purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

description: The SOS response in E. coli is an inducible DNA repair system activated by DNA damage. When replication forks stall at DNA lesions, single-stranded DNA accumulates and is coated by RecA protein, forming RecA-ssDNA nucleoprotein filaments. These filaments stimulate auto cleavage of the LexA repressor, derepressing over 40 SOS genes involved in DNA repair, cell division arrest, and mutagenic bypass. The system includes nucleotide excision repair (UvrABC), recombinational repair (RecA, RecBCD), error-prone polymerases (Pol II, IV, V), and cell division inhibitors (SulA). This flowchart illustrates the complete pathway from DNA damage detection through RecA activation, LexA cleavage, SOS gene expression, DNA repair processes, and system recovery when damage is resolved.

graph TD
    A[DNA Damaging Agents] -->|UV Light| B[Thymine Dimers]
    A -->|Chemicals| C[DNA Adducts]
    A -->|Ionizing Radiation| D[Double-Strand Breaks]
    
    B --> E{Replication Fork Encounter?}
    C --> E
    D --> E
    
    E -->|Fork Stalling| F[ssDNA Accumulation]
    E -->|No Damage| G[Normal Replication]
    
    F --> H[SSB Protein Binding]
    H --> I[RecA Protein]
    I --> J{RecA Loading?}
    
    J -->|RecFOR Pathway| K[RecA-ssDNA Filament]
    J -->|RecBCD Pathway| L[RecA Loading at DSBs]
    
    K --> M[Active RecA Nucleoprotein]
    L --> M
    
    M --> N[/LexA Repressor/]
    N --> O{LexA Auto-Cleavage?}
    O -->|RecA-Stimulated| P[LexA Cleavage]
    O -->|No Signal| Q[LexA Intact]
    
    Q --> R[/SOS Genes Repressed/]
    P --> S[/LexA Degradation/]
    S --> T[/Derepression of SOS Regulon/]
    
    T --> U{SOS Gene Expression?}
    U -->|Early Genes| V[recA lexA]
    U -->|Middle Genes| W[uvrABC recN dinI]
    U -->|Late Genes| X[umuDC sulA]
    
    V --> Y[Enhanced RecA Production]
    Y --> Z[Positive Feedback Loop]
    Z --> M
    
    V --> AA[Enhanced LexA Production]
    AA --> AB[Negative Feedback]
    
    W --> AC{DNA Repair Pathways?}
    AC -->|Excision Repair| AD[UvrABC Endonuclease]
    AC -->|Recombinational Repair| AE[RecA RecBCD RecN]
    AC -->|Base Excision| AF[AlkA Tag]
    
    AD --> AG[Damage Recognition UvrA2B]
    AG --> AH[Dual Incision UvrC]
    AH --> AI[UvrD Helicase]
    AI --> AJ[Gap Filling Pol I]
    AJ --> AK[Ligase Sealing]
    AK --> AL[Repaired DNA]
    
    AE --> AM[Homologous Recombination]
    AM --> AN[RecBCD Resection]
    AN --> AO[RecA-Mediated Strand Invasion]
    AO --> AP[Holiday Junction Formation]
    AP --> AQ[RuvABC Resolution]
    AQ --> AL
    
    X --> AR{Cell Division Control?}
    AR -->|SulA Expression| AS[/FtsZ Inhibition/]
    AS --> AT[Cell Division Arrest]
    AT --> AU[Filamentation]
    
    X --> AV{Error-Prone Repair?}
    AV -->|UmuDC Pol V| AW[Translesion Synthesis]
    AV -->|DinB Pol IV| AX[Lesion Bypass]
    
    AW --> AY{Mutagenic Bypass?}
    AX --> AY
    AY -->|Successful| AZ[Replication Restart]
    AY -->|Mutations Introduced| BA[Adaptive Mutagenesis]
    
    BA --> BB[Genetic Diversity]
    BB --> BC[Potential Adaptation]
    
    AL --> BD{DNA Damage Resolved?}
    AZ --> BD
    BD -->|Yes| BE[ssDNA Depletion]
    BD -->|No| F
    
    BE --> BF[RecA Filament Disassembly]
    BF --> BG[Loss of LexA Cleavage Signal]
    BG --> BH[LexA Accumulation]
    BH --> BI[/SOS Gene Repression/]
    
    BI --> BJ[/SulA Degradation by Lon/]
    BJ --> BK[FtsZ Release]
    BK --> BL[Cell Division Resumption]
    
    BI --> BM[Return to Basal State]
    BM --> BN{Cell Fate?}
    BN -->|Repair Successful| BO(\1)
    BN -->|Excessive Damage| BP[Cell Death]
    
    BO --> BQ[Normal Growth]
    BP --> BR[Apoptosis-like Death]
    
    W --> BS{Additional Functions?}
    BS -->|DinI| BT[RecA Modulation]
    BS -->|RecN| BU[DSB Repair Enhancement]
    BS -->|Cho| BV[Nucleoid Condensation]

name: E. coli SOS Response
notes: Created based on classic and contemporary research on the E. coli SOS response, including RecA-LexA regulation and DNA repair mechanisms.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on extensive peer-reviewed literature on the E. coli SOS response, including studies on RecA-LexA regulation, DNA repair pathways, and mutagenesis mechanisms. The pathway components, regulatory interactions, and logic gates have been validated against primary research sources. The model represents the canonical bacterial SOS response that serves as a paradigm for stress-induced mutagenesis and DNA repair.

sources.title: The SOS response: recent insights into umuDC-dependent mutagenesis and DNA damage tolerance
Regulation of the Escherichia coli SOS response
